tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.1), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.1 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Companion HUD overlay: transparent always-on-top panel, state/checklist/route/callout rows, click-through (issue #47)
tests.requires: `[hud].enabled` (off by default; applies LIVE, no restart). Needs Doug's desktop - cannot run offline/headless. Run ED borderless/windowed so an always-on-top window can float over it (full-screen exclusive can cover any overlay - expected).
tests.expected: Toggling the HUD on (Settings or voice) shows a small transparent top-right always-on-top panel; the state row tracks Listening/Thinking/Speaking/Idle; the checklist row shows next pending item + count and advances on a mark; the route row shows jumps-to-dest + scoopable and decrements/arrives; the callout row shows the last callout; clicks pass through to the game behind; it never blocks startup and logs no error when off.

steps.text: Toggle on - Settings page: flip Companion HUD overlay on the Settings page -> a small panel appears top-right, background fully transparent (desktop/game shows through), staying on top.
Toggle on - voice: with the HUD off, say 'turn the HUD on' -> the panel appears (settings-by-voice path). 'Turn the HUD off' -> it disappears. Toggling is live (no restart).
Voice-loop state row: hold PTT -> the state row tracks Listening -> Thinking -> Speaking -> Idle as you talk and COVAS replies.
Checklist row: with a checklist loaded, the row shows your next pending item + count (e.g. '... (2/10 done)'); mark it done by voice -> the row advances to the next pending item.

Route row: plot a multi-jump route (writes NavRoute.json) -> the row shows 'N jumps to <dest>'; lock the next jump -> it appends scoopable / NOT scoopable; each jump decrements the count; arrival shows 'Arrived at <dest>'.
Callout row: trigger a proactive or route callout -> the last-callout row shows that line.
Click-through (Windows): move the mouse over the panel and click -> the click lands on the window/game behind it (the HUD is non-interactive).
Fail-soft: it never blocks startup or the voice loop; with `[hud].enabled = false` no window appears and nothing is logged as an error.

tests.title: VR HUD overlay: in-headset SteamVR overlay, no-dep/no-SteamVR fail-soft, placement/size, both surfaces (issue #48)
tests.requires: `[hud].vr_enabled` (off by default; independent of the 2D HUD). Cannot run offline/headless - needs a VR headset, SteamVR, and the optional dep. Setup: `pip install openvr`, start SteamVR, and run Elite Dangerous in VR through SteamVR (native SteamVR headset, or Quest via Link/Air Link/Virtual Desktop in SteamVR mode).
tests.expected: Without openvr the app starts, the VR overlay simply doesn't appear, and it logs `openvr unavailable` (no crash); with openvr but no SteamVR it logs `SteamVR not available` and continues; with both running the same four-row HUD floats in the headset and tracks live state/checklist/route/callout data; world vs head placement and vr_width_m behave; both 2D + VR surfaces can show at once; Quest-via-SteamVR shows it, native Oculus/OpenComposite does not (expected).

steps.text: No-dep fail-soft (do this first, no headset needed): with openvr NOT installed, set `[hud].vr_enabled = true` -> the app starts normally, the VR overlay doesn't appear, and the log notes `openvr unavailable` (a hud info line) - no crash, and the 2D HUD (if on) still works.
No-SteamVR fail-soft: with openvr installed but SteamVR not running, enable the VR HUD -> it logs `SteamVR not available` and continues; nothing appears; the voice loop is unaffected.

Toggle on - Settings page: with SteamVR + ED-in-VR running, flip VR HUD overlay on the Settings page -> the panel appears floating in the headset at a readable size, showing the state/checklist/route/callout rows.
Toggle on - voice: say 'turn the VR HUD on' -> the overlay appears; 'turn the VR HUD off' -> it disappears. Live, no restart.

Live content: confirm the in-headset rows track the same live data as the 2D HUD - hold PTT and watch the state row; a loaded checklist shows the next item; a plotted route shows jumps-remaining (+ scoopable); a callout fills the callout row.
Placement - world vs head: with `[hud].vr_placement = "world"` the panel stays cockpit-fixed as you turn your head; switch to `"head"` (re-enable to recreate the overlay) and it follows your view.
Size: adjust `[hud].vr_width_m` (e.g. 0.4 vs 0.8) -> the panel's physical width changes accordingly; pick a comfortable, legible size.
Both surfaces at once: enable both `[hud].enabled` and `[hud].vr_enabled` -> the desktop window and the headset overlay show simultaneously and independently.

Quest boundary (if applicable): confirm the overlay shows on Quest via SteamVR (Link/Air Link/Virtual Desktop-SteamVR), and does NOT on the native Oculus runtime / OpenComposite (expected - use OpenKneeboard/OVR Toolkit to capture the 2D window there).
